content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Review Agent — Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis
2	
3	You are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) maintain the blueprint markers that require *semantic* judgement (`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations), and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	`\leanok` placement is handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs immediately before you. **Do not add or remove `\leanok` yourself.** If a `\leanok` you expect is missing, the underlying Lean source still has a sorry or doesn't compile — investigate, don't paper over.
6	
7	**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs.** You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts as part of your analysis. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint-marker files.
8	
9	## Iteration number — canonical
10	
11	Your invocation prompt contains `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. As of 2026 these are the **same number** — `session_M/` is always the review of `iter-NNN` (i.e. M == NNN). Use the iteration form (`iter-NNN`) when referencing the iteration in prose (`summary.md` metadata, recommendations titles like "Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-{NNN+1:03d})") and the bare integer (`session_M`) when referring to the review-output directory. If older `summary.md` / `recommendations.md` files use the legacy independent session counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents — your new file uses the iteration number.
12	
13	## Step 1: Identify Context
14	
15	1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` — count existing session folders to determine the current session number.
16	2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.
17	3. Run `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` to see what changed.
18	
19	## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)
20	
21	**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.
22	
23	The file contains:
24	
25	- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)
26	- Remaining lines: One event per tool call — edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds
27	
28	**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text → new_text).
29	**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.
30	**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.
31	**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.
32	
33	This is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.
34	
35	## Step 3: Read Recent History
36	
37	If previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
38	
39	## Step 4: Write Proof Journal
40	
41	Create the session folder and write two files:
42	
43	```bash
44	mkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>
45	```
46	
47	### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`
48	
49	Must include:
50	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)
51	- For EACH target attempted:
52	  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point
53	  - What was learned from each failed attempt
54	  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas
55	- Key findings / proof patterns discovered
56	- Recommendations for next session
57	
58	### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`
59	
60	Each line MUST follow this JSON format — one entry per target theorem:
61	
62	```json
63	{
64	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
65	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
66	  "target": {
67	    "file": "path/to/File.lean",
68	    "theorem": "theorem_name"
69	  },
70	  "session": {
71	    "id": "session_N",
72	    "model": "model-name"
73	  },
74	  "findings": {
75	    "blocker": "description if blocked",
76	    "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1", "lemma2"]
77	  },
78	  "attempts": [
79	    {
80	      "attempt": 1,
81	      "strategy": "what was tried",
82	      "code_tried": "actual Lean code or tactic",
83	      "line_number": "line number in the initial file where this code was attempted",
84	      "lean_error": "actual error message if failed",
85	      "goal_before": "the goal state before this attempt",
86	      "goal_after": "the goal state after this attempt",
87	      "result": "success|failed|partial",
88	      "insight": "what was learned from this attempt"
89	    }
90	  ],
91	  "next_steps": "..."
92	}
93	```
94	
95	**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:
96	- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded
97	- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)
98	- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one
99	
100	### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`
101	
102	Write concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:
103	- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized
104	- Which approaches showed promise but need more work
105	- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)
106	- Any reusable proof patterns discovered
107	
108	If your analysis shows the prover has hit the exact same blocker for several consecutive iterations on the same target, you should explicitly instruct the Plan Agent to avoid retrying the same approach without putting more effort into understanding the underlying issue.
109	
110	## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md (Knowledge Base only)
111	
112	The per-session narrative (Overall Progress: total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, this session's analysis) goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` — see the next subsection. PROJECT_STATUS.md carries only the cumulative Knowledge Base:
113	
114	```markdown
115	# Project Status
116	
117	## Knowledge Base
118	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
119	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
120	
121	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
122	- <target>: <reason>
123	
124	## Last Updated
125	<ISO timestamp>
126	```
127	
128	If an existing PROJECT_STATUS.md still carries an "Overall Progress" section from a prior project that grew it, leave the old content where it is (do not delete history) but stop appending to it. New session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`.
129	
130	### Per-iteration sidecars (split write)
131	
132	Your invocation prompt contains a `## Per-iteration sidecars` block that names `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` as the destination for THIS session's narrative. Split your writes accordingly:
133	
134	- **The per-session "Overall Progress" narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**, NOT to PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Overall Progress" section. PROJECT_STATUS.md no longer carries a session log.
135	- **You DO keep updating PROJECT_STATUS.md's "Knowledge Base" section.** Cumulative non-obvious facts (errors not to reproduce, reusable proof patterns, Mathlib idioms that worked) still belong in the Knowledge Base. The Knowledge Base is the only growing-but-curated part of PROJECT_STATUS.md.
136	- **Format for `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`**: same fields as the Overall Progress block above (Total sorry, branches closed, solved/partial/blocked/untouched, plus any narrative analysis). Born-bounded — this file contains THIS session only, never a multi-session log.
137	- Older `iter/iter-MMM/review.md` files are on disk for you to read on demand, but the recent window is already injected in your prompt.
138	
139	## Step 6: Blueprint Markers
140	
141	`\leanok` placement is now handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs between the prover and you. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration in the Lean source, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. **Do not touch `\leanok` markers yourself.** If you see one missing where you expect it, the underlying file probably doesn't compile or still has a sorry — do not paper over that with a manual edit.
142	
143	Your remaining marker responsibilities are the ones that require semantic judgement:
144	
145	- **`\mathlibok`** (statement-block only) — declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias / direct reference). The deterministic script never adds or removes this; you decide based on the prover's task result.
146	- **`\lean{...}` macro maintenance** — if a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `\lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.
147	- **`% NOTE: <reason>` annotations** — when a block is unformalized because the informal statement did not translate cleanly, add a `% NOTE: ...` comment explaining the obstacle so the plan agent sees it.
148	- **Stripping `\notready`** — if a `\notready` marker still sits on a block that the prover has now landed, remove it. The deterministic script does not manage `\notready`.
149	
150	### `\mathlibok` rules (your domain)
151	
152	Add `\mathlibok` inside the statement block when:
153	- The Lean side references a Mathlib name directly (`def foo := Mathlib.bar`, `theorem foo := Mathlib.bar`, or a simple `export Mathlib.Foo (bar)`), AND
154	- The Archon-side declaration itself contains no `sorry` and introduces no new proof obligation.
155	
156	If you add `\mathlibok`, no `\leanok` is needed on the proof block — the deterministic script will leave the proof block alone if there's no Lean proof body to verify.
157	
158	### Record what you changed
159	
160	In `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated" section listing **only the changes you personally made** (the deterministic script's `\leanok` adds/removes are committed separately as `archon[NNN/marker-sync]` and don't need to appear here):
161	
162	```markdown
163	## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
164	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
165	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: added `% NOTE: prover reported translation gap, see task_results/Core.md`
166	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
167	- `Core.tex`, `thm:foo`: corrected `\lean{Old.foo}` → `\lean{New.foo}` after refactor rename
168	```
169	
170	### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`
171	
172	While the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. 
173	
174	**Always check the planner's iter sidecar for escalations.** Read `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` (this iter's plan-agent narrative). If the planner intentionally skipped provers — signaled by the `(no prover dispatch this iter ...)` marker in `PROGRESS.md`'s `## Current Objectives` section, by a "user escalation" / "hard gate fired" notice in the sidecar, or by the planner's `planValidate.status: ok_intentional_skip` meta — you MUST write a concise alert banner to `TO_USER.md` naming:
175	- What the impasse is (one sentence).
176	- What options the planner has surfaced (one bullet per option, if applicable).
177	- Where to find full context (the sidecar path).
178	
179	The planner does NOT write to `USER_HINTS.md` for escalations — `USER_HINTS.md` is user-to-planner only. The escalation banner is your responsibility; you are the user-facing channel.
180	
181	**Rules:**
182	- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).
183	- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.
184	
185	## Step 7: Dispatch review subagents
186	
187	You may dispatch read-only review subagents BEFORE writing your consolidated summary. They are audits whose findings you incorporate into `summary.md` / `recommendations.md`. Each runs as its own fresh-context Claude process; spawning two or more in one assistant message runs them in parallel, subject to the global `max_parallel` cap.
188	
189	### Where the catalog comes from
190	
191	Your invocation prompt contains an auto-generated **Available subagents** section listing every enabled descriptor. Reviewers are typically marked `[read-only]`. A descriptor tagged `[MANDATORY]` for this phase MUST be dispatched before the phase completes. Do not `ls .archon/subagents/` — the catalog you were handed is authoritative.
192	
193	When you decide to invoke a specific reviewer, read its full prompt and directive shape from `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
194	
195	### How to dispatch
196	
197	Pattern (Bash tool, parallel-able, run **in foreground** so the report lands before you write your summary):
198	
199	```
200	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
201	  --name <subagent-name> \
202	  --slug <kebab-case-slug> \
203	  --directive-file .archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md \
204	  --write-domain 'task_results/**'
205	```
206	
207	Each directive must be **fully self-contained** — the reviewer reads only what the directive points it at. The directive shape for each reviewer is documented in its descriptor under `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
208	
209	### When NOT to dispatch
210	
211	- If this session was a pure proof-filling round with no new definitions or refactors, skip reviewers. They add latency and cost for no value.
212	- If a previous session in the last 3 iters already ran the same audit on the same scope, don't repeat unless that scope changed.
213	- The plan agent may also have dispatched reviewers proactively — check `task_results/` for matching reports before doubling up.
214	
215	### Incorporating findings
216	
217	After the reviewers return, read every report. Land each finding in your `summary.md` and `recommendations.md`:
218	
219	- **CRITICAL / HIGH** findings → bullet at the top of `recommendations.md` with the suggested action.
220	- **MEDIUM** findings → bullet in `recommendations.md`'s body.
221	- **LOW** findings → one-liner in `summary.md`'s notes section.
222	
223	Do NOT repeat the full report content in your summary — link to the report file. The plan agent reads your summary, not the raw reports.
224	
225	## Step 8: Self-Validation
226	
227	After writing all files, validate your output by checking:
228	- [ ] milestones.jsonl has valid JSON on every line
229	- [ ] Each milestone has `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`
230	- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has at least 1 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`
231	- [ ] Number of attempts per milestone is proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`
232	- [ ] summary.md includes specific code/errors, not just high-level summaries
233	- [ ] recommendations.md includes actionable next steps
234	- [ ] You did NOT add or remove any `\leanok` marker yourself (those are handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you)
235	- [ ] For every Mathlib-backed declaration the prover reported, the blueprint chapter has `\mathlibok` (your domain)
236	- [ ] Any `\lean{...}` macro rename flagged in a `task_results/<file>.md` has been applied
237	- [ ] No `\notready` marker remains on a block whose Lean declaration now exists
238	
239	## Permissions
240	
241	You may write to:
242	- `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
243	- `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`
244	- `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` — markers (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`), `\lean{...}` macro corrections, stale-marker cleanup, and `% NOTE:` comments. Do NOT rewrite the informal prose — that is the plan agent's surface.
245	
246	You must NOT write to:
247	- Any `.lean` files
248	- `.archon/PROGRESS.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
249	- `.archon/task_pending.md` or `.archon/task_done.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
250	- Blueprint informal content (theorem/proof prose, chapter structure) — only the markers and `% NOTE:` comments are yours.

93	## Rebuttal to strategy-critic Alt 2 (temporary-axiom pile-composition smoke test)
94	
95	The strategy-critic-iter135 proposes that the iter-143 plan agent
96	dispatch an intermediate prover lane that attempts the M2.a body
97	closure with piece (iii) **axiomatised** (project-local axiom matching
98	the eventual piece (iii) signature, *removed* once piece (iii) lands),
99	to smoke-test pieces (i.b)+(i.c)+(ii) composition into the rigidity
100	body 8 iter earlier than the M2.a body closure lane.
101	
102	**Rebuttal (explicit per plan.md § "Detecting and responding to stuck
103	routes" requirement that strategy-critic verdicts must be acted on or
104	explicitly rebutted)**:
105	
106	1. **The iter-121 user directive bans new axioms.** The strategy-critic
107	   notes the rule may be read too strictly, arguing that a temporary
108	   smoke-test axiom that is introduced and removed in the same iter
109	   does not close M2.a. But the iter-121 user directive does not have a
110	   "same-iter removal" carve-out — its operative language is
111	   "**no named axioms**" as the end-state, with the project's autonomous
112	   loop "operating as a Mathlib contributor". An axiom that exists in
113	   the tree at *any* point in the loop's history is auditable; a
114	   reviewer asked "did this project ever introduce an axiom" would
115	   answer yes, even after removal. The cost is in user trust, not just
116	   in kernel cleanliness.
117	
118	2. **The iter-135 type-elaboration check already serves as a
119	   lighter-weight integration spike.** The strategy-critic's CHALLENGE 1
120	   (1-iter type-check spike for piece (i.b)) is fully satisfied by the
121	   iter-135 mathlib-analogist's `lean_run_code` verification + the
122	   Wave-2 refactor's actual landing of the intended-type signatures.
123	   The same pattern can be applied for pieces (i.c) and (ii) when they
124	   come into scope (a mathlib-analogist dispatch + an honest sorry-
125	   bodied scaffold landing). That gives the same "do the pieces
126	   compose at the type level" signal as the temporary-axiom smoke
127	   test, without introducing any axiom.
128	
129	3. **The strategy-critic's argument is real but the cost asymmetry
130	   tilts the wrong way.** A failure detected at iter-143 (via temporary
131	   axiom) vs. iter-151 (via real piece (iii)) saves 8 iter of build,
132	   per the strategy-critic. But: the iter-143 plan agent would have to
133	   stage the temporary-axiom infrastructure + commit it + remove it
134	   + audit the removal — each step a new failure surface. The
135	   iter-135 plan agent's judgment is that the lighter-weight
136	   type-elaboration check (analogist + sorry-bodied scaffolds, no
137	   axioms) gives 80% of the integration signal for 20% of the
138	   process cost. The full-strength integration signal (the actual
139	   composition compiles) is only available at the real iter-151+
140	   close anyway.
141	
142	4. **The CONVERGING verdict on Route 1 + the 1-CHALLENGE SOUND verdict
143	   on the rest of the strategy reinforces this.** The iter-135
144	   progress-critic + strategy-critic are aligned that the strategy is
145	   healthy; iter-143 is far enough out that a deeper-think trigger
146	   then can reconsider, but the iter-135 pre-commitment is not to
147	   schedule a temporary-axiom lane.
148	
149	**Iter-136 strategy-critic should NOT re-issue Alt 2.** If iter-136's
150	strategy-critic does re-issue it, the iter-136 plan agent should ratify
151	this iter-135 rebuttal in the iter-136 sidecar rather than re-litigate
152	in the absence of new evidence.

225	
226	- **Binary-product shear-iso closure recipe**
227	  *(NEW iter-134 Archon canonical — surfaced + codified by the iter-134
228	  prover lane on `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight` at
229	  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:349`)*:
230	  When closing the inverse-pair `hom_inv_id` / `inv_hom_id` proofs of a
231	  binary-product shear iso `σ : G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` in a Cartesian monoidal
232	  category `C` over `GrpObj G` data (with `σ.hom := lift (fst G G) μ`,
233	  `σ.inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)`), the
234	  Mathlib template `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight`'s bulk `simp_only`
235	  chain DOES NOT transfer — the binary-product version has 2 free
236	  inputs (`fst`, `snd`) where `mulRight` has 1, so `comp_lift_assoc`
237	  matches too eagerly and the goal stays out of `lift_fst_snd`-reducible
238	  form. Recipe that works:
239	  ```lean
240	  apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
241	  · simp                                                 -- fst coord
242	  · rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]
243	    rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]
244	    rw [← Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]
245	    -- Goal: `lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ = snd G G`
246	    -- (or symmetric `lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ) ≫ μ = snd G G`
247	    --  for inv_hom_id; positions of `μ` and `ι` flip)
248	    rw [show (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G) =
249	        lift (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (fst G G) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from by
250	          rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp],
251	      CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left,           -- or `lift_comp_inv_right`
252	      MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]
253	  ```
254	  **Key insight**: structural rewrites (`Category.id_comp`, `lift_snd`,
255	  `comp_lift`, `lift_fst`) must precede the `lift_lift_assoc` rewrite,
256	  AND the inner `μ`-expansion to `lift (fst G G) (snd G G) ≫ μ` must
257	  happen at exactly the right pattern position via a single `show`-
258	  equation (NOT `conv_lhs`, which matches both occurrences of `μ`).
259	  This NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL contribution candidate (Mathlib has no
260	  packaged binary-product shear iso) can be upstreamed once stable. ~30
261	  LOC per inverse-pair, kernel-only axioms.

263	- **The `Nonempty (X ≅ X)` tautological-placeholder anti-pattern (DO NOT REPEAT)**
264	  *(NEW iter-134 Archon canonical NEGATIVE finding — surfaced by both
265	  `lean-auditor-review134` and
266	  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review134` as
267	  must-fix-this-iter on 3 load-bearing declarations in
268	  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476/508/566`)*:
269	  When the prover lane needs to land a named declaration for a
270	  multi-iter Mathlib-gap target (e.g., a deferred sheaf-level base-change
271	  iso), **DO NOT** ship the body as
272	  `theorem foo : Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩` with the intended
273	  substantive type recorded only in the docstring. This pattern:
274	  - Compiles cleanly with kernel-only axioms (because `Iso.refl _` is
275	    a real term, not `sorry`).
276	  - Emits **zero machine-readable incompleteness signal** —
277	    `sorry_analyzer`, `lean_diagnostic_messages`, and `sync_leanok` all
278	    classify the declaration as complete; the deterministic
279	    `\leanok`-sync phase will add `\leanok` to the blueprint's proof
280	    block because the Lean compiles.
281	  - Triggers the strict rubric of `lean-auditor` + `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`
282	    as "weakened-wrong-definition + signature mismatch + excuse-comment"
283	    on the load-bearing declaration. `\notready` on the blueprint side
284	    + intended-type prose in the docstring **does not** clear the
285	    rubric.
286	  - Leaves the dependency surface broken: parameters like
287	    `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom]`, `[IsProper G.hom]`,
288	    `[GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]` declared on the placeholder are
289	    **unused** by `Iso.refl _`, a Lean-side smoke signal of the
290	    body/type mismatch (Lean's unused-variable hints fire under strict
291	    settings).
292	  **PREFERRED PATTERN** when a multi-iter Mathlib-gap target needs a
293	  scaffold: write the **intended** type in the signature and ship the
294	  body as `sorry`. Mirrors `Jacobian.lean:194-215`'s
295	  `positiveGenusWitness` honest scaffold (audited iter-134 by
296	  `lean-auditor-review134` and classified as honest). Example, taking
297	  `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` from iter-134:
298	  ```lean
299	  -- BAD (iter-134 anti-pattern):
300	  theorem mulRight_globalises_cotangent (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) ... :
301	      Nonempty (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ≅
302	                Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom) :=
303	    ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
304	
305	  -- GOOD (honest scaffold):
306	  noncomputable def mulRight_globalises_cotangent
307	      (G : Over (Spec (.of k))) ... :
308	      Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ≅
309	        (PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)).obj
310	          ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_η G)).obj
311	            (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)) :=
312	    sorry
313	  ```
314	  The honest scaffold:
315	  - Emits a downstream-visible `sorry` (sorry counter increments).
316	  - Pins the intended signature for the iter-135+ prover lane to fill in.
317	  - Uses the declared instance parameters (because the intended type
318	    references them through `PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)`).
319	  - Aligns with the project's iter-127-onward scaffold convention
320	    (`genusZeroWitness` iter-127, `nonempty_jacobianWitness` Phase-C,
321	    `rigidity_over_kbar` iter-126, `positiveGenusWitness` iter-134).
322	  **Codified rule**: NEVER ship `Nonempty (X ≅ X)` as a stand-in for a
323	  substantive iso. If the iter cannot produce the intended type
324	  signature (because the supporting infrastructure — e.g. `φ_*`
325	  compatibility morphisms — does not yet exist), then either (a)
326	  produce the supporting infrastructure in the same iter, or (b) DEFER
327	  the declaration entirely until the iter that can produce it. Do not
328	  introduce a hollow placeholder declaration into the environment.

360	  *(NEW iter-133 Archon canonical — surfaced by

363	  When the project needs to express `mulRight`-equivariance of a
364	  cotangent-like construction on a smooth group scheme `G/k`, the
365	  iter-133 mathlib-analogist recommends phrasing the RHS at the
366	  **`PresheafOfModules` (sheaf) level** — `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf
367	  G.hom ≅ (PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)).obj ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_η G)).obj (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom))`

373	  creating a chart-set cocycle concern at the value level. Phrasing
374	  the RHS at the sheaf-of-modules level puts the σ-iso composition
375	  entirely at the presheaf-of-modules level where the chart non-
376	  canonicity does not enter, AND decouples the (chart-localisation

390	- **`Classical.choose`-chain parallel extraction for rank lemmas against opaque body shapes**
391	  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical — the technique that closed
392	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity_finrank_eq` in ~40 LOC after 4 iters of
393	  body-shape work)*:
394	  When the body of a `noncomputable def` is built from a chain of
395	  `Classical.choose` / `Classical.choose_spec` extractions on an
396	  existential `h : ∃ X, P X`, a downstream rank-style lemma whose goal
397	  targets the body's underlying carrier (e.g. `Module.finrank k (someDef G) = n`)
398	  can be proven by **reconstructing the same chain locally inside the
399	  lemma's proof**. Because the same `Classical.choose` call (with the
400	  same arguments to `h`) produces the *same* term (Lean's definitional
401	  equality is reflexive on `Classical.choose`-applied-to-the-same-
402	  proof), the goal's `someDef G` and the proof's
403	  `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…])` form share the
404	  same underlying carrier — exposable via `change` to the explicit
405	  `TensorProduct …` form via `ModuleCat.ExtendScalars.obj'`'s
406	  definitional unfolding. Recipe:
407	  ```lean
408	  let h := Scheme.smooth_locally_free_omega (n := n) G.hom x₀
409	  set U : (Spec (.of k)).Opens := h.choose with hU_def
410	  set V : G.left.Opens := h.choose_spec.choose with hV_def
411	  set e : V ≤ G.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose with he_def
412	  have hxV : x₀ ∈ V := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.1
413	  have hfree : Module.Free … := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.1
414	  have hrank : Module.rank … = ↑n := h.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.2.2.2
415	  -- (install algebra structures via letI, ψV via let, Nontrivial via haveI …)
416	  change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct … k Ω[…]) = n
417	  rw [Module.finrank_baseChange (R := k) (S := …) (M' := Ω[…])]
418	  exact Module.finrank_eq_of_rank_eq hrank
419	  ```
420	  This avoids the cost of building a `_eq_extendScalars`-style strong
421	  acceptance lemma + `obtain ⟨U, V, e, htop, heq⟩ := acceptance` +

422	  `rw [heq]` chain (which iter-131 also installs in
423	  `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:198` as a defensive
424	  alternative). **Key insight**: `Classical.choose` is opaque but
425	  *deterministic on equal proofs*; two `Classical.choose`-chains on
426	  the same existential reduce to definitionally-equal terms. This is
427	  precisely the structural property the iter-131 body refactor
428	  delivered (per iter-131 mathlib-analogist
429	  `analogies/cotangent-body-shape.md`); iter-132 is the closure
430	  test that proved the design works for downstream rank lemmas.

451	- **`change` vs `show` for definitional-equality carrier shifts**
452	  *(NEW iter-132 Archon canonical — Lean linter warning surfaced during
453	  the iter-132 rank-lemma close, resolved by swapping to `change`)*:
454	  When a goal's carrier (e.g. `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G : ModuleCat k`)
455	  is *definitionally equal* but not syntactically equal to a target
456	  form (e.g. `TensorProduct Γ(G,V) k Ω[…]`), use `change` rather than
457	  `show`. The Lean linter actively warns on `show` when it observes
458	  the tactic actually shifting the goal:
459	  > The `show` tactic should only be used to indicate intermediate
460	  > goal states for readability. However, this tactic invocation
461	  > changed the goal.
462	  `change` is the right primitive for genuine def-eq carrier shifts;
463	  `show` is reserved for verbatim restatement to aid readability.
464	  Both tactics share the same semantic effect (def-eq goal swap) but
465	  Lean treats them as structurally different. Iter-132 instance:
466	  `show Module.finrank k (TensorProduct …) = n` → warning;
467	  `change Module.finrank k (TensorProduct …) = n` → silent. Both
468	  closed the proof; only `change` is linter-clean.

470	- **`Classical.choice (α := X) ?_` is the standard escape for "Type-valued `def` body depending on a `Prop`-level existential", but it discards content**
471	  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical — surfaced as the critical iter-130
472	  finding flagged by `lean-auditor-review130` must-fix #1)*:
473	  When a `def` returning a `Type` (e.g. `ModuleCat k`) needs to use data
474	  from a `Prop`-level existential `∃ U V, P U V`, the elaborator
475	  forbids `obtain ⟨U, V, h⟩ := proof` directly with
476	  `recursor Exists.casesOn can only eliminate into Prop`. The standard
477	  workaround:
478	  ```lean
479	  refine Classical.choice (α := X) ?_
480	  obtain ⟨U, V, h⟩ := proof  -- now legal: Prop goal context Nonempty X
481	  exact ⟨theTermYouWant U V h⟩
482	  ```
483	  **The result is NOT definitionally equal to `theTermYouWant U V h`** —
484	  it is some unspecified inhabitant of `X` chosen by `Classical.choice`.
485	  Only `Nonempty X` is structurally recoverable; any downstream lemma
486	  about the structure of the inhabitant CANNOT be proved by unfolding
487	  the `def` and applying a fact about `theTermYouWant U V h`. **Use
488	  `Classical.indefiniteDescription` / chained `Classical.choose` /
489	  `Classical.choose_spec`** if any downstream proof needs to access
490	  the destructured data. Iter-130 instance:
491	  `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` body wrapped the
492	  explicit chart-base-changed Kähler module
493	  `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψV.hom).obj (ModuleCat.of … Ω[…⁄…])` in
494	  `Classical.choice (α := ModuleCat k)`. The body type-checks, passes
495	  `lean_verify` with kernel-only axioms, AND satisfies the
496	  progress-critic's vacuity-rule-out acceptance test (references
497	  `smooth_locally_free_omega`, 40 LOC, not simp-only) — yet the
498	  iter-129+ rank lemma `finrank cotangentSpaceAtIdentity G = n` is
499	  **unprovable against this body** for the same structural reason
500	  iter-128's was. **Lesson**: in addition to "kernel-clean ≠
501	  mathematically correct" (iter-129 lesson #2), kernel-clean +
502	  acceptance-test-pass also do not guarantee structural-content-
503	  delivery to downstream consumers. A `Type`-valued `def` whose body
504	  uses an outer `Classical.choice` wrapper is opaque to definitional
505	  unfolding; rank/free/finite/dimension claims do not transport
506	  through it. **Documented at
507	  `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_130/summary.md` and §
508	  recommendations.md CRITICAL block.**

510	- **Lattice symbols (`⊤`, `⊥`, `⊓`, `⊔`) cannot be part of identifiers in tactic blocks**
511	  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical — surfaced as a parse-error during
512	  the iter-130 body swap)*:
513	  Hypothesis names like `h⊤` parse partially in tactic blocks — Lean
514	  tokenises the `⊤` as the lattice top symbol and aborts the parse
515	  with `unexpected token '⊤'; expected ':=' or '|'`. Use ASCII-only
516	  identifiers (`htop`, `hbot`, `hsup`, `hinf`) for hypothesis names in
517	  tactic blocks. This is distinct from term-mode definitions (where
518	  `⊤` survives, with surrounding whitespace), and from operator
519	  notations (where the symbol is a valid token). **Documented at

522	- **The unique-point collapse for `Spec` of a field: `(s : ↥(Spec (.of k)))` is always `default` via `Subsingleton.elim`**
523	  *(NEW iter-130 Archon canonical — surfaced during the iter-130 body
524	  swap when establishing `(⊤ : (Spec (.of k)).Opens) ≤ ηleft ⁻¹ᵁ V`)*:
525	  For `k` a field, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`

529	  ↥(Spec (.of k))` and an open `U ⊆ Spec (.of k)` containing the

531	  ```lean

542	- **Presheaf-pullback at the top open of `Spec k` collapses to `k`; evaluating a presheaf-of-modules pullback along a `Spec k → X` morphism at `⊤_X` discards the `X`-side structure**
543	  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical — surfaced as a critical CORRECTION
544	  to iter-128 pattern #2; recorded by `mathlib-analogist-lieAlgebra-rank-bridge-iter129`)*:
545	  For `f : X ⟶ Spec k` (so `f.base : X.topology → Spec k`),
546	  `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullback CommRingCat f.base).obj S.presheaf).obj (op ⊤)`
547	  is by definition the pointwise left Kan extension colimit over the
548	  indexing diagram `{V : Opens(Spec k) | f.base(⊤_X) ⊆ V}`. Since
549	  `Spec k` is single-point (only opens are `∅` and `⊤`), the indexing
550	  diagram has a unique element `V = ⊤_{Spec k}`, and the colimit
551	  collapses to `Γ(Spec k, ⊤) ≅ k` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. **Consequence
552	  for the iter-128 `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` construction**:
553	  `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom` evaluated at `op ⊤` is, via the
554	  `rfl` identification `relativeDifferentials'_obj`, the Kähler module
555	  of `φ'.app (op ⊤) : k → Γ(G, ⊤)`. For smooth proper geometrically
556	  irreducible `G/k`, smoothness ⇒ geometrically reduced ⇒ geometrically
557	  integral ⇒ `Γ(G, ⊤) = k` (Stacks 0BUG; Mathlib's
558	  `AlgebraicGeometry.isField_of_universallyClosed`). So
559	  `φ'.app (op ⊤) : k → k` is essentially the identity, and
560	  `KaehlerDifferential.subsingleton_of_surjective` gives `Ω[k/k] = 0`;
561	  the iter-128 body `(ModuleCat.extendScalars ψ.hom).obj 0 = 0` (the

564	  global-sections form of a presheaf-of-modules pullback) is
565	  **correct as an algebraic construction but mathematically degenerate**
566	  for the cotangent-at-identity application because the proper geom-irr
567	  hypothesis forces all global sections to land in `k`. Anyone needing

578	- **Soundness lesson: kernel-clean does not imply mathematically correct**
579	  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical — surfaced when the iter-128
580	  `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` body, certified kernel-only by `lean_verify`
581	  with `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`, was discovered to
582	  compute the zero `k`-module for the target class)*:
583	  `lean_verify <decl>` returning only kernel axioms certifies that the
584	  **proof** uses only kernel-allowed axioms (no `sorryAx`, no project
585	  axiom, no named-axiom escalation). It does **not** certify that the
586	  **statement** is mathematical content as intended. Definitions
587	  whose bodies type-check can encode the zero object, the empty type,
588	  or any other vacuous construction when the math claims something
589	  substantive. **Rule for future scaffolding rounds**: when a new
590	  declaration's Lean construction differs structurally from the
591	  blueprint's proof sketch (here: evaluate-then-extend-scalars on a
592	  *presheaf* vs the `𝔪/𝔪²` *stalk* presentation the blueprint
593	  diagrams), dispatch a `mathlib-analogist` consult **before** locking
594	  the construction into a refactor — even if `lake build` and
595	  `lean_verify` are both clean. The iter-128 review-phase
596	  `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review128` flagged the
597	  bridge gap abstractly; the iter-129 analogist consult sharpened it
598	  to a concrete vacuity claim. **Lesson**: file-vs-blueprint bridges
599	  that exist in prose ("the bridge will be a future lemma") must be
600	  treated as load-bearing requirements that may not exist in Mathlib;
601	  if a `mathlib-analogist` cannot name the bridge, the construction
602	  needs revision. **Documented at `analogies/lieAlgebra-rank-bridge.md`
603	  Decision 1 + `proof-journal/sessions/session_129/summary.md` § Key findings.**
604	
605	- **`Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` is the canonical Mathlib name** (not `IsRegularLocalRing.cotangentSpace`)
606	  *(NEW iter-129 Archon canonical — minor; surfaced when the iter-129
607	  strategy-critic + blueprint-reviewer flagged the phantom name)*:
608	  The local-ring cotangent space `𝔪/𝔪²` is `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace R`
609	  in Mathlib `b80f227`, defined as `(maximalIdeal R).Cotangent` in
610	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Cotangent`. The names
611	  `IsRegularLocalRing.cotangentSpace` and `LocalRing.cotangentSpace`
612	  do **not** exist — `lean_local_search "cotangentSpace"` returns
613	  only `Algebra.Generators.cotangentSpaceBasis`,
614	  `Algebra.Extension.CotangentSpace`, and
615	  `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace`. For the regular-local-ring
616	  finrank claim, the bridge is `IsRegularLocalRing` →
617	  `Ideal.IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace` (not via `IsRegularLocalRing`'s
618	  own namespace). **Replace any future blueprint or strategy prose
619	  citing the phantom name.** Caught + corrected in iter-129's
620	  `RigidityKbar.tex` rewrite by `blueprint-writer-rigiditykbar-iter129`.

# Session 134 — review of iter-134

## Metadata

- **Iteration**: 134 (canonical Archon iter)
- **Project sorry count**: 3 → 4 (per-file: `Jacobian.lean` 2 → 3 via the plan-phase Wave-2 `positiveGenusWitness` scaffold refactor; `RigidityKbar.lean` 1 unchanged; `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` 0 unchanged; **the iter-134 prover lane introduced 0 new sorries** — it added 4 substantively closed declarations plus 3 hollow placeholders typed as `Nonempty (X ≅ X)`)
- **Targets attempted (prover lane)**: 1 — `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.mulRight_globalises_cotangent` (piece (i.b) main lemma)
- **Targets adjacent (introduced by the lane, blueprint-side or refactor)**: 6 — `shearMulRight` (closed), `shearMulRight_hom_fst` / `_hom_snd` (`@[simps]` companions), `schemeHomRingCompatibility` (closed helper), 2 placeholder helper lemmas (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`, `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`)
- **Stage**: stays at `prover` for iter-135
- **Axiom hygiene**: kernel-only across all new declarations (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`)
- **Lean file growth**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` 296 → 574 LOC (+278 LOC, within both the 600-LOC trigger arm of `strategy-critic-iter134` CHALLENGE 1 and the 210–440 LOC piece-(i.b) envelope)

## Outcome at a glance

- **Prover lane shipped PARTIAL.** Step 1 of piece (i.b) (the binary-product shear iso `shearMulRight` with its two `@[simps]` companions + the `schemeHomRingCompatibility` packaging helper) is **substantively closed**. Steps 2 + 3 + the main composition (the load-bearing `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two` ~150–300 LOC NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL + the `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section` ~30–80 LOC + the main lemma `mulRight_globalises_cotangent`) are shipped as **placeholders**: body `⟨Iso.refl _⟩`, type `Nonempty (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ≅ Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom)`. The intended sheaf-level RHS using `PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)` / `PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_η G)` is documented in each declaration's docstring but NOT in the signature.
- **Both review-phase audits flagged the placeholder pattern as `must-fix-this-iter`** under the strict rubric (weakened-wrong-definition + signature mismatch + excuse-comment), with explicit acknowledgement that the convention is `\notready`-disclosed in `RigidityKbar.tex` and was explicitly pre-committed by the iter-134 plan agent. Both reports defer the project-convention question to the plan agent — see `recommendations.md` for the iter-135 PRIMARY DECISION on whether to refactor the placeholder bodies to intended-type-with-`sorry` (the auditor's recommended fix) or tighten the rubric to exempt `\notready`-marked + docstring-intended-type placeholders for multi-iter Mathlib-gap work.
- **One project-health smell flagged by the auditor (major)**: the 3 placeholder theorems carry `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]` parameters that are **unused** by the `Iso.refl _` body — a Lean-side smoke signal of the body/type mismatch.
- **One blueprint coverage gap surfaced by the checker (major)**: `shearMulRight` is a substantive ~30 LOC NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL contribution candidate, but `RigidityKbar.tex` describes it only inline inside the proof of `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` — there is no dedicated `\begin{lemma}\label{lem:GrpObj_shearMulRight}\lean{...}` block. The auxiliary pointer chapter `AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex` lists it but the main mathematical chapter does not.
- **Plan-phase Wave-2 refactor lane landed cleanly**: `refactor-positiveGenusWitness-scaffold-iter134` added the `positiveGenusWitness C (hg : 0 < genus C)` scaffold at `Jacobian.lean:194–215` (~21 LOC; sorry body; M3 off-critical-path; per-file sorry count 2 → 3; project sorry count 3 → 4). The `lean-auditor-review134` audit classified this as an **honest scaffold** with no excuse-comments — the type signature is the intended type and the deferral to M3 is correctly attributed.
- **Project compile**: clean. `lean_diagnostic_messages` returns 0 items on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean` at iter-134 close (verified at the end of the prover lane). No regression on previously-closed declarations.

## Pre-processed attempt data

`/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` contains 94 events from the iter-134 prover lane (2026-05-17T19:27 → 19:47Z): 8 edits to `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`, 11 diagnostic checks, 1 goal state, 6 lemma searches, plus 7 `lean_verify` axiom-hygiene checks. Reconstructed attempt arc:

1. **Reading / locating** (events 1–11): prover read `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`, `Differentials.lean`, the `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp_.lean` shear-iso template, `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Cartesian.Mon_.lean` for `lift_lift_assoc`, `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pullback`, `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf` for `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`; located `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (Grp_.lean:277-281), `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left` (Grp_.lean:185), `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_right` (Grp_.lean:173); discovered `MonObj.lift_lift_assoc` template.

6. **Final shape Edit** (events 62–63): the resolved shape — `apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext; · simp; · rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, lift_snd]; rw [← Category.assoc, comp_lift]; rw [← Category.assoc, lift_fst, lift_snd]; rw [show (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) μ ≫ μ : G ⊗ G ⟶ G) = lift (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (fst G G) ≫ μ) (snd G G) ≫ μ from by rw [MonObj.lift_lift_assoc, lift_fst_snd, Category.id_comp], CategoryTheory.GrpObj.lift_comp_inv_left, MonObj.lift_comp_one_left]` — closed both `hom_inv_id` and (symmetrically with `lift_comp_inv_right`) `inv_hom_id`. **Diagnostic**: clean.
7. **Identifier scoping Edit** (events 76–78): unscoped `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` failed under `autoImplicit := false` (event 77); fixed by qualifying as `Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf`. Final diagnostic clean.
8. **Axiom verification** (events 82–88): `lean_verify` on `shearMulRight`, `schemeHomRingCompatibility`, `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`, `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`, `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` — all kernel-only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## Per-target detail

### Target 1 (substantively closed): `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight`

- **Location**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:349`.
- **Type**: `(G : C) [GrpObj G] → G ⊗ G ≅ G ⊗ G` in any Cartesian monoidal category `C`.
- **Body**: `hom := lift (fst G G) μ`; `inv := lift (fst G G) (lift (fst G G ≫ ι) (snd G G) ≫ μ)`; both `hom_inv_id` and `inv_hom_id` closed via the `lift_lift_assoc` + `lift_comp_inv_left/right` + `lift_comp_one_left` chain documented in attempt 3 of `milestones.jsonl`.
- **Companion `@[simps]`-spawned simp lemmas**: `shearMulRight_hom_fst : σ.hom ≫ fst G G = fst G G` and `shearMulRight_hom_snd : σ.hom ≫ snd G G = μ` (lines 386, 391; `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]`).
- **Significance**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL contribution candidate per the iter-133 mathlib-analogist Decision 1. The Mathlib template `CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight` (`Monoidal/Grp_.lean:277-281`) packages only the one-input automorphism `a ↦ a · f` for a fixed global element `f`; the project's binary-product version takes both factors of `G ⊗ G` as free inputs.
- **Verification**: `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight` returns kernel-only axioms `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

### Target 2 (substantively closed): `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.schemeHomRingCompatibility`

- **Location**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:417`.
- **Type**: `{Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ Z) → (TopCat.Presheaf.pullback (C := CommRingCat) f.base).obj Z.presheaf ⟶ Y.presheaf`.
- **Body**: `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c`.
- **Significance**: packages the adjunction transpose `φ' = ((adj).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c` once so that the iter-135+ placeholder helpers can refer to a uniform compatibility-morphism API for `PresheafOfModules.pullback`-flavored statements. Mirrors the `φ'` already used by `Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf` at `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean:52`.

### Target 3 (PLACEHOLDER — must-fix per audits): `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`

- **Location**: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476`.

# Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-135)

## PRIMARY DECISION — convention vs. rubric on `Nonempty (X ≅ X)` placeholder bodies

Both mandatory review-phase audits flagged the iter-134 prover lane's three placeholder declarations at `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476/508/566` (`relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two`, `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_restrict_along_identity_section`, `mulRight_globalises_cotangent`) as **must-fix-this-iter under the strict rubric**:

- `lean-auditor-review134`: 3 critical-severity must-fix items + 3 critical-severity excuse-comments. Reports the pattern as "weakened-wrong-definition: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in" on load-bearing declarations.
- `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-cotangent-grpobj-review134`: 3 must-fix-this-iter under strict rubric. Reports the pattern as "signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose"; flags that `\leanok` was added by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase to the proof blocks of these three lemmas (because the Lean compiles with no `sorry`) but is "technically-consistent but semantically misleading" — the closed `proof` matches a tautology, not the intended substantive statement.

The iter-134 plan agent **explicitly pre-committed** to this placeholder pattern (PROGRESS.md `## Current Objectives` does not name the convention, but the prover's task result records it as honored), and the blueprint blocks in `RigidityKbar.tex` correctly carry `\notready`. So the pattern is fully disclosed, not a hidden defect. But it emits **zero machine-readable incompleteness signal**: `sorry_analyzer` cannot distinguish a tautological-iso placeholder from a real closure, and a downstream consumer of `mulRight_globalises_cotangent` sees a named, signed theorem that returns a hollow `Iso.refl`.

**The iter-135 plan agent must make a PRIMARY DECISION**:

- **OPTION (i) — TIGHTEN THE RUBRIC** to exempt `\notready`-marked + docstring-intended-type placeholders for multi-iter Mathlib-gap work, formalizing the placeholder convention in `STRATEGY.md` and/or `.archon/CLAUDE.md` so future auditor / checker dispatches don't re-flag.
- **OPTION (ii) — REFACTOR THE LEAN** to carry the intended sheaf-level signatures with `sorry` bodies (mirroring the honest-scaffold pattern of `Jacobian.lean`'s `positiveGenusWitness` at L194–215, which **both** review-phase audits classified as **honest** because the type is the intended type and the `sorry` emits a downstream-visible incompleteness signal).

**Recommendation: ADOPT OPTION (ii).** Refactoring delivers four concrete benefits the auditor surfaced:

1. The unused `{n : ℕ} [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n G.hom] [IsProper G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]` parameters on the three placeholders (currently triggering Lean's unused-variable hints under strict settings) become USED once the body type matches the intended `PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)`-based RHS.
2. `sorry_analyzer` then sees the iter-134-introduced incompleteness and reports it consistently — both `sync_leanok` and the project's whole-project sorry-counting commands then correctly classify the placeholder pieces as incomplete (currently they read as "complete with kernel-only axioms" because `Iso.refl _` is a real term, not `sorry`).
3. The pattern aligns with the iter-127-onward project convention (per `positiveGenusWitness` iter-134, `genusZeroWitness` iter-127, `nonempty_jacobianWitness` Phase-C scaffold, `rigidity_over_kbar` iter-126) where multi-iter deferred work is encoded as `theorem foo : <intended-type> := sorry` or `noncomputable def foo : <intended-type> := sorry`, never as a tautological stand-in.
4. The iter-135 prover lane on Step 2 (the load-bearing 150–300 LOC body) can then directly replace the `sorry` body for `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_basechange_along_proj_two` against its *intended* type, without first re-shaping the signature.

The cost of Option (ii) is one refactor-lane dispatch (~3 declarations × ~10–20 LOC of signature replacement = ~30–60 LOC; sorry count 4 → 7 temporarily until the iter-135+ prover work lands). The refactor lane should be dispatched parallel-with the iter-135 mandatory critics so the prover lane that follows hits the corrected signatures.

A concrete refactor-subagent directive for Option (ii) (suggested):

```
target: AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean lines 449–572 (3 placeholder theorems)

For each of the 3 placeholders, replace:
  theorem foo (args) : Nonempty (Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ≅
      Scheme.relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩
with:
  noncomputable def foo (args) : <intended type per the docstring + blueprint>
      := sorry

For `mulRight_globalises_cotangent`:
  intended type per blueprint `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` lines 298–305:
    relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom ≅
      (PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_str G)).obj
        ((PresheafOfModules.pullback (φ_η G)).obj
          (relativeDifferentialsPresheaf G.hom))
  where φ_str : compatibility morphism for `G.hom : G.left ⟶ Spec (.of k)`
        φ_η  : compatibility morphism for `η[G].left : Spec (.of k) ⟶ G.left`
  Both via `((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat _).homEquiv _ _).symm <structure-sheaf comorphism>` — i.e., `schemeHomRingCompatibility G.hom` and
  `schemeHomRingCompatibility η[G].left`, both already provided in-file at line 417.

Same shape for the 2 helpers (intended types in blueprint lines 384–399 + 427–441).

123	- **Weakened-wrong definitions**: a `def Foo := <something structurally different>` standing in for the real concept (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic …` when the real definition is an invertible quasi-coherent sheaf).
124	- **Parallel APIs of existing Mathlib**: a project file that copy-and-modifies a Mathlib definition (changing one type-class or argument) instead of instancing the existing Mathlib version. Bridge lemmas needed to consume it are the symptom.

* The plan agent invokes this wrapper directly; the wrapper sees no

* When a subagent (e.g. coordinator) spawns a child via Bash, the
  parent subagent's slug is exported in env by ``Subagent.run``; the
  wrapper picks it up and forwards.

Iteration number comes from ``ARCHON_ITER_NUM`` (set by the loop's
plan phase). The script fails loudly if it's missing rather than
silently defaulting, because a wrong iter_num would route the JSONL
log to the wrong directory.

Informally: the relative cotangent of $G$ is canonically isomorphic to the constant $\mathcal O_{G}$-module with fibre $\eta_{G}^{*}\,\Omega_{G/k}$, trivialised along the structure map.

  \paragraph{Iter-127 over-k risk register.} The globalisation \textbf{must} be formulated functorially via the shear scheme map $\sigma$ — which is a morphism in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$, valid for any base field — and \textbf{not} as a pointwise translation by closed (or $\bar k$-rational) points of $G$. The pointwise-translation argument requires either alg-closure of $k$ or a Nullstellensatz-style $\bar k$-point lifting (as in Mathlib's \texttt{pointEquivClosedPoint}, used in the alg-closed-base branch of \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Smooth.lean:50--51}); the functorial shear formulation sidesteps that obstacle entirely. The iter-133 mathlib-analogist verdict (\texttt{analogies/mulright-globalises-cotangent.md} Decision 4) explicitly rules out the pointwise idioms of \texttt{Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.GroupLieAlgebra.mulInvariantVectorField} (requires \texttt{[Group G]}) and \texttt{Mathlib.Topology.Algebra.Group.Basic.Homeomorph.shearMulRight} (requires \texttt{[Group G]} on the carrier) as the over-k-forbidden alternatives.

  \paragraph{Lean target name vs.\ construction (option (i), iter-133).} The named target \texttt{mulRight\_globalises\_cotangent} retains the \texttt{mulRight} naming convention because the shear iso $\sigma = \langle \pr_{1}, \mu \rangle$ on $G \times_{k} G$ is the binary-product upgrade of \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281}, which packages a one-input automorphism $a \mapsto a \cdot f$ for a fixed global element $f \colon \mathbf{1} \to G$): the map $\sigma$ parametrises the family of right-translations $\{\mathrm{mulRight}\,a\}_{a \in G}$ globally across $G$ by taking the first coordinate as the parameter, and the second projection $\pr_{2} \circ \sigma = \mu$ realises the right-translation action on the second coordinate. The "globalises the cotangent" phrasing in the name records that this family-of-translations is what triviallises $\Omega_{G/k}$ via pullback along $\sigma$ (cf.\ the inverse-pair proof shape of \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback}, \texttt{Grp\_.lean:293--323}, which uses the structurally identical \texttt{lift \dots (lift \dots \,\iota\, \dots) ≫ μ} construction). No standalone \texttt{mulRight}-name function appears in the Lean signature itself; the connection is purely nomenclatural, and the underlying Lean construction is the binary-product shear iso, not a one-input \texttt{mulRight} automorphism.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:GrpObj_cotangentSpace, lem:GrpObj_shearMulRight, lem:GrpObj_omega_basechange_proj, lem:GrpObj_omega_restrict_to_identity_section}


\leanok
  \emph{Step 1: Build the shear iso $\sigma$.} By \cref{lem:GrpObj_shearMulRight}, the binary-product shear $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} = \langle \pr_{1}, \mu \rangle \colon G \times_{k} G \to G \times_{k} G$ in $\Over\,(\Spec k)$ — informally $(a, b) \mapsto (a, a \cdot b)$ — is a self-iso of $G \times_{k} G$ with explicit inverse $\sigma_{\mathrm{inv}} = \langle \pr_{1}, \mu \circ (\iota \circ \pr_{1}, \pr_{2}) \rangle$, and satisfies the companion identifications $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_{1} = \pr_{1}$ and $\sigma_{\mathrm{hom}} \circ \pr_{2} = \mu$. The Lean target is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.shearMulRight} (closed iter-134, $\sim 30$ LOC of \texttt{lift\_lift\_assoc} / \texttt{lift\_comp\_inv\_*} / \texttt{lift\_comp\_one\_left} calculus on the $\GrpObj$ axioms, modelled on \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.mulRight} and \texttt{CategoryTheory.GrpObj.isPullback} of \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Grp\_.lean:277--281,\,293--323}).

(the iter-135 honest scaffold for piece (i.b) Step 3, ~30–80 LOC
closure).

success confirms the iter-135 honest-scaffold pattern + the

closure.

markers on the proof blocks of the iter-134 placeholder lemmas

- `RigidityKbar.tex:402` — proof block of `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises`

convenience)

  *(NEW iter-135 Archon canonical POSITIVE finding — the workflow
  that resolved 3 must-fix-this-iter findings from iter-134 review
  audits cleanly in 1 plan-only iter)*:
  When a prior iter has shipped a load-bearing target as
  `theorem foo : Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩` (the iter-134
  anti-pattern; see the related Knowledge Base entry above), the
  canonical iter-N+1 remediation is a refactor lane that:
  1. **Dispatches a `mathlib-analogist` first** to verify (via
     `lean_run_code` elaboration check) that the intended substantive
     RHS type elaborates cleanly against the current project state.
     This is decision (D) of a 4-part analogist verdict structure
     ((A) which Mathlib idiom to use; (B) literal Lean text for the
     intended types; (C) whether to introduce new project `def`s for
     the compatibility morphisms; (D) `lean_run_code` elaboration
     check). Produces a persistent file at `analogies/<slug>.md`.
  2. **Lands a refactor lane** swapping each placeholder
     `theorem ... : Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩` to a
     `noncomputable def ... : <intended-substantive-RHS> := sorry`.
     The signature is now what the math claims; the `sorry` body
     emits a downstream-visible incompleteness signal that
     `sorry_analyzer`, `lean_diagnostic_messages`, and `sync_leanok`
     can all see.
  3. **Has no prover dispatch in the refactor iter**
     (`planValidate.status: ok_intentional_skip`,
     `prover.durationSecs: 0`). The next iter dispatches the prover
     lane against the corrected signatures.
  4. **Codifies the convention** in a section docstring at the
     refactor's edit site naming the canonical compatibility-morphism
     idiom (in the iter-135 instance: `Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom`
     for `PresheafOfModules.pullback`, contrasted with the
     adjunction-transpose helper) so future iters cannot accidentally
     re-introduce the placeholder pattern.
  **Iter-135 instance**: closed 3 must-fix-this-iter findings (3
  weakened-wrong placeholders at `Cotangent/GrpObj.lean:476/508/566`
  → 3 honest sorry-bodied scaffolds at L468/L496/L560) in 1
  plan-only refactor + writer iter. Sorry count 4 → 6 — but BOTH

  improvement" (`lean-auditor` verbatim verdict). The 4 → 6 increase

  health delta: materially positive.** **Documented at
